context: Tests whether the agent writes commit messages in conventional commits format with correct type prefixes and imperative mood descriptions. Six different types of changes are provided to trigger the full range of commit types.

checklist.name: Format: type colon description
checklist.description: Every commit message starts with a type prefix followed by a colon and space before the description (e.g., 'feat: ...' or 'fix(scope): ...')

checklist.name: feat type for new feature
checklist.description: The PDF invoice download endpoint change uses 'feat' as the commit type

checklist.name: fix type for bug fix
checklist.description: The notification service crash fix uses 'fix' as the commit type

checklist.name: docs type for README
checklist.description: The README update uses 'docs' as the commit type

checklist.name: refactor type for extraction
checklist.description: The authentication logic extraction uses 'refactor' as the commit type

checklist.name: test type for new tests
checklist.description: The unit tests addition uses 'test' as the commit type

checklist.name: chore type for build script
checklist.description: The build script update uses 'chore' as the commit type

checklist.name: Imperative mood
checklist.description: All six commit descriptions use imperative mood (e.g., 'add', 'fix', 'update') rather than past tense (e.g., 'added', 'fixed', 'updated') or gerunds (e.g., 'adding')

checklist.name: No invalid types
checklist.description: No commit message uses a type that is not one of: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test
